{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. [[国立国会図書館]] '''{{Cite book|和書|author=伊勢新聞社編|coauthors=|title=伊勢年鑑.昭和17年|publisher=伊勢新聞社|year=1941|month=10|url={{NDLDC|1069778}}|ref=伊勢年鑑1942}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=皇国敬神会|year=1922|month=12|title=全国有名神社御写真帖|chapter=神宮(内宮、外宮)|publisher=皇国敬神会|url={{NDLDC|966854/7}}|ref=全国有名神社写真}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1925|month=3|url=|ref=神宮便覧(大正14)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1928|month=10|url=|ref=神宮便覧(昭和3)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=大神宮叢書. 第3 後篇|publisher=西濃印刷岐阜支店|year=1936|month=11|url={{NDLDC|1239755}}|ref=大神宮叢書.第3後篇}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=中村徳五郎|editor=|year=1921|month=7|title=皇大神宮史|chapter=第三節　相殿及び別宮|publisher=弘道閣|url={{NDLDC|943761/167}}|ref=皇大神宮史}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=経済雑誌社編|coauthors=|chapter=倭姫命世紀|title=国史大系.第7巻|publisher=経済雑誌社|year=1898|month=8|url={{NDLDC|991097/253}}|ref=国史大系七}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=日本旅行協會編纂|coauthors=|title=伊勢参宮案内|publisher=日本旅行協会|year=1930|month=2|url={{NDLDC|1022598}}|ref=伊勢参宮案内}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=広池千九郎|editor=|year=1915|month=9|title=伊勢神宮と我国体|chapter=第二十章　別宮并に摂社及び末社|publisher=日月社|url={{NDLDC|954644/76}}|ref=広池国体}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1937|month=7|title=神道大辞典　第一巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913333}}|ref=平凡辞典1937一巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1939|month=6|title=神道大辞典　第二巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913348}}|ref=平凡辞典1939二巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1940|month=9|title=神道大辞典　第三巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913359}}|ref=平凡辞典1940三巻}} == See Also == * [[トヨウケビメ]] * [[荒魂・和魂]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} * {{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} * [[伊勢市]] == External Links == {{commonscat|Taka-no-miya (Ise Shrine)}} * [http://www.isejingu.or.jp/geku_2.html 多賀宮(たかのみや) - 伊勢神宮] * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0210.html 多賀宮（たかのみや）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たかのみや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[ja:多賀宮]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社|大たかのみや]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:2013年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. 伊勢神宮] * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0210.html 多賀宮（たかのみや）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たかのみや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[ja:多賀宮]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社|大たかのみや]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:2013年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. 伊勢神宮] * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0210.html 多賀宮（たかのみや）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たかのみや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[ja:多賀宮]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社|大たかのみや]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:2013年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[ja:多賀宮]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考資料 == * 『お伊勢まいり』（発行：伊勢神宮崇敬会） *{{Cite book|和書|author=上田正昭ほか|authorlink=上田正昭|editor=|year=1988|month=11|chapter=別宮の祭祀|title={{small|神宮の展開}}　伊勢の大神|publisher=筑摩書房|series=|isbn=4-480-85469-X|ref=筑摩展開}} * 『宇治山田市史　上巻』（宇治山田市役所編、昭和4年発行、昭和63年復刻、国書刊行会発行） *{{Cite book|和書|author1=こほりくにを|authorlink1=小堀邦夫|author2=日竎貞夫|editor=|year=1996|month=8|chapter=|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=保育社|series=カラーブックス890|isbn=4-586-50890-6|ref=保育社カラー}} *{{Cite book|和書|author={{small|神宮禰宜}}桜井勝之進|authorlink=桜井勝之進|editor=|year=1988|month=5|chapter=五　度会の神々|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=学生社|series=日本の神社|isbn=4-311-40704-1|ref=桜井1988伊勢}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=矢野憲一|authorlink=矢野憲一|editor=|year=2006|month=11|chapter=|title=伊勢神宮　{{small|知られざる杜のうち}}|publisher=角川書店|series=角川選書402|isbn=4-04-703402-9|ref=矢野、杜}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ 　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション] - [[国立国会図書館]] '''{{Cite book|和書|author=伊勢新聞社編|coauthors=|title=伊勢年鑑.昭和17年|publisher=伊勢新聞社|year=1941|month=10|url={{NDLDC|1069778}}|ref=伊勢年鑑1942}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=皇国敬神会|year=1922|month=12|title=全国有名神社御写真帖|chapter=神宮(内宮、外宮)|publisher=皇国敬神会|url={{NDLDC|966854/7}}|ref=全国有名神社写真}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1925|month=3|url=|ref=神宮便覧(大正14)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1928|month=10|url=|ref=神宮便覧(昭和3)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=大神宮叢書. 第3 後篇|publisher=西濃印刷岐阜支店|year=1936|month=11|url={{NDLDC|1239755}}|ref=大神宮叢書.第3後篇}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=中村徳五郎|editor=|year=1921|month=7|title=皇大神宮史|chapter=第三節　相殿及び別宮|publisher=弘道閣|url={{NDLDC|943761/167}}|ref=皇大神宮史}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=経済雑誌社編|coauthors=|chapter=倭姫命世紀|title=国史大系.第7巻|publisher=経済雑誌社|year=1898|month=8|url={{NDLDC|991097/253}}|ref=国史大系七}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=日本旅行協會編纂|coauthors=|title=伊勢参宮案内|publisher=日本旅行協会|year=1930|month=2|url={{NDLDC|1022598}}|ref=伊勢参宮案内}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=広池千九郎|editor=|year=1915|month=9|title=伊勢神宮と我国体|chapter=第二十章　別宮并に摂社及び末社|publisher=日月社|url={{NDLDC|954644/76}}|ref=広池国体}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1937|month=7|title=神道大辞典　第一巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913333}}|ref=平凡辞典1937一巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1939|month=6|title=神道大辞典　第二巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913348}}|ref=平凡辞典1939二巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1940|month=9|title=神道大辞典　第三巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913359}}|ref=平凡辞典1940三巻}} == See Also == * [[トヨウケビメ]] * [[荒魂・和魂]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} * {{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} * [[伊勢市]] == External Links == {{commonscat|Taka-no-miya (Ise Shrine)}} * [http://www.isejingu.or.jp/geku_2.html 多賀宮(たかのみや) - revision.text:
{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[ja:多賀宮]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考資料 == * 『お伊勢まいり』（発行：伊勢神宮崇敬会） *{{Cite book|和書|author=上田正昭ほか|authorlink=上田正昭|editor=|year=1988|month=11|chapter=別宮の祭祀|title={{small|神宮の展開}}　伊勢の大神|publisher=筑摩書房|series=|isbn=4-480-85469-X|ref=筑摩展開}} * 『宇治山田市史　上巻』（宇治山田市役所編、昭和4年発行、昭和63年復刻、国書刊行会発行） *{{Cite book|和書|author1=こほりくにを|authorlink1=小堀邦夫|author2=日竎貞夫|editor=|year=1996|month=8|chapter=|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=保育社|series=カラーブックス890|isbn=4-586-50890-6|ref=保育社カラー}} *{{Cite book|和書|author={{small|神宮禰宜}}桜井勝之進|authorlink=桜井勝之進|editor=|year=1988|month=5|chapter=五　度会の神々|title=伊勢神宮|publisher=学生社|series=日本の神社|isbn=4-311-40704-1|ref=桜井1988伊勢}} *{{Cite book|和書|author=矢野憲一|authorlink=矢野憲一|editor=|year=2006|month=11|chapter=|title=伊勢神宮　{{small|知られざる杜のうち}}|publisher=角川書店|series=角川選書402|isbn=4-04-703402-9|ref=矢野、杜}} *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/ 　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション] - [[国立国会図書館]] '''{{Cite book|和書|author=伊勢新聞社編|coauthors=|title=伊勢年鑑.昭和17年|publisher=伊勢新聞社|year=1941|month=10|url={{NDLDC|1069778}}|ref=伊勢年鑑1942}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=皇国敬神会|year=1922|month=12|title=全国有名神社御写真帖|chapter=神宮(内宮、外宮)|publisher=皇国敬神会|url={{NDLDC|966854/7}}|ref=全国有名神社写真}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1925|month=3|url=|ref=神宮便覧(大正14)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1928|month=10|url=|ref=神宮便覧(昭和3)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=大神宮叢書. 第3 後篇|publisher=西濃印刷岐阜支店|year=1936|month=11|url={{NDLDC|1239755}}|ref=大神宮叢書.第3後篇}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=中村徳五郎|editor=|year=1921|month=7|title=皇大神宮史|chapter=第三節　相殿及び別宮|publisher=弘道閣|url={{NDLDC|943761/167}}|ref=皇大神宮史}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=経済雑誌社編|coauthors=|chapter=倭姫命世紀|title=国史大系.第7巻|publisher=経済雑誌社|year=1898|month=8|url={{NDLDC|991097/253}}|ref=国史大系七}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=日本旅行協會編纂|coauthors=|title=伊勢参宮案内|publisher=日本旅行協会|year=1930|month=2|url={{NDLDC|1022598}}|ref=伊勢参宮案内}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=広池千九郎|editor=|year=1915|month=9|title=伊勢神宮と我国体|chapter=第二十章　別宮并に摂社及び末社|publisher=日月社|url={{NDLDC|954644/76}}|ref=広池国体}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1937|month=7|title=神道大辞典　第一巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913333}}|ref=平凡辞典1937一巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1939|month=6|title=神道大辞典　第二巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913348}}|ref=平凡辞典1939二巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1940|month=9|title=神道大辞典　第三巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913359}}|ref=平凡辞典1940三巻}} == See Also == * [[トヨウケビメ]] * [[荒魂・和魂]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} * {{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} * [[伊勢市]] == External Links == {{commonscat|Taka-no-miya (Ise Shrine)}} * [http://www.isejingu.or.jp/geku_2.html 多賀宮(たかのみや) - 伊勢神宮] * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0210.html 多賀宮（たかのみや）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たかのみや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[ja:多賀宮]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社|大たかのみや]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:2013年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. [[国立国会図書館]] '''{{Cite book|和書|author=伊勢新聞社編|coauthors=|title=伊勢年鑑.昭和17年|publisher=伊勢新聞社|year=1941|month=10|url={{NDLDC|1069778}}|ref=伊勢年鑑1942}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=皇国敬神会|year=1922|month=12|title=全国有名神社御写真帖|chapter=神宮(内宮、外宮)|publisher=皇国敬神会|url={{NDLDC|966854/7}}|ref=全国有名神社写真}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1925|month=3|url=|ref=神宮便覧(大正14)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=神宮便覧|publisher=中央公論社|year=1928|month=10|url=|ref=神宮便覧(昭和3)}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=神宮司庁編|coauthors=|title=大神宮叢書. 第3 後篇|publisher=西濃印刷岐阜支店|year=1936|month=11|url={{NDLDC|1239755}}|ref=大神宮叢書.第3後篇}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=中村徳五郎|editor=|year=1921|month=7|title=皇大神宮史|chapter=第三節　相殿及び別宮|publisher=弘道閣|url={{NDLDC|943761/167}}|ref=皇大神宮史}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=経済雑誌社編|coauthors=|chapter=倭姫命世紀|title=国史大系.第7巻|publisher=経済雑誌社|year=1898|month=8|url={{NDLDC|991097/253}}|ref=国史大系七}} '''{{Cite book|和書|author=日本旅行協會編纂|coauthors=|title=伊勢参宮案内|publisher=日本旅行協会|year=1930|month=2|url={{NDLDC|1022598}}|ref=伊勢参宮案内}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=広池千九郎|editor=|year=1915|month=9|title=伊勢神宮と我国体|chapter=第二十章　別宮并に摂社及び末社|publisher=日月社|url={{NDLDC|954644/76}}|ref=広池国体}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1937|month=7|title=神道大辞典　第一巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913333}}|ref=平凡辞典1937一巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1939|month=6|title=神道大辞典　第二巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913348}}|ref=平凡辞典1939二巻}} '''{{Citation |和書|author=|editor=平凡社|year=1940|month=9|title=神道大辞典　第三巻|chapter=|publisher=平凡社|url={{NDLDC|1913359}}|ref=平凡辞典1940三巻}} == See Also == * [[トヨウケビメ]] * [[荒魂・和魂]] * {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} * {{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} * [[伊勢市]] == External Links == {{commonscat|Taka-no-miya (Ise Shrine)}} * [http://www.isejingu.or.jp/geku_2.html 多賀宮(たかのみや) - 伊勢神宮] * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0210.html 多賀宮（たかのみや）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たかのみや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[ja:多賀宮]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社|大たかのみや]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:2013年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available. 伊勢神宮] * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0210.html 多賀宮（たかのみや）] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|たかのみや}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[ja:多賀宮]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社|大たかのみや]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:2013年竣工の日本の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:aka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taka-no-Miya]][[Category:qq]]

{{神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Takanomiya Shrine in Toyouke Grand Shrine.JPG|256px]] |名称=多賀宮 |所在地=[[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]279 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 7.4 | 経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 13.8 |祭神=[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神荒魂]] |社格=[[式内社]]（大）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}別宮 |創建=[[804]]以前 |本殿=[[神明造]] |例祭= |ラベル位置 = left |地図 = Japan Mie }} '''Taka-no-Miya''' ('''多賀宮''') is a sub-shrine (betsugu) within the precincts of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in [[豊川町 (伊勢市)|Toyokawa Town]], [[伊勢市|Ise City]], [[三重県|Mie Prefecture]][[Taka-no-Miya#平凡辞典1939二巻|神道大辞典(平凡1939)二巻]]コマ246(原本422頁)[[Taka-no-Miya#桜井1988伊勢|伊勢神宮(学生社)]]75-77頁『多賀宮』。 In the 62nd {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} ([[2013年|2013]]), the {{ill|8=ja|9=遷御|relocation}} was carried out on October 13 after {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} in the separate palace。{{cite news|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131014181310/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|title=内宮別宮で遷御の儀|publisher=[[読売新聞]]|date=2013年10月11日|accessdate=2013-10-15|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1015-0127-42/www.yomiuri.co.jp/e-japan/mie/news/20131011-OYT8T00088.htm|archivedate=2013-10-15}} == Overview == [[image:Ise Shrine - Taka no miya2.jpg|thumb|200px|Haiden (Worship Hall)]] [[image:Taka-no-miya 02.jpg|thumb|200px|Stone steps leading to {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}]] [[image:外宮別宮多賀宮新旧社殿.JPG|thumb|200px|New and old shrine buildings constructed during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}}]] '''{{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}''' (Taka-no-miya) is located on the southern side of the {{ill|Geku|ja|外宮}}'s main sanctuary, atop '''Hinoki-Oyama''' (檜尾山). The term "{{ill|Betsugu|ja|別宮}}" means "separate shrine," indicating that it is revered just after the main sanctuary. In addition to Taka-no-Miya, the other betsugu of Geku include {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}} (Tsuchi-no-miya) and {{ill|Kaze-no-Miya|ja|風宮}} (Kaze-no-miya) within the precincts, and {{ill|Tsukiyomi-no-Miya (Geku)|ja|月夜見宮|lt=Tsukiyomi-no-Miya}} (Tsukiyomi-no-Miya) outside the precincts[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]123頁『豊受大神別宮四所』, making a total of four. Among these, Taka-no-Miya is the oldest and the only one recorded in both the "Toyo'uke-no-Miya Ritual Record" (804) and the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#筑摩展開|筑摩、神宮の展開]]51-54頁『別宮の内実』 Since the enshrined deity is the {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂}} of {{ill|Toyouke-Omikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, Taka-no-Miya holds the highest status among the four betsugu.。 After passing the '''{{ill|Kameishi|ja|亀石}}''' (Turtle Stone) beside the pond in front of the main sanctuary[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]55頁『亀石』, visitors ascend 98 stone steps located between Tsuchi-no-Miya and Kaze-no-Miya to reach Taka-no-Miya.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]67頁『別宮・多賀宮への石段』 The Turtle Stone is said to have been transported from the entrance rock of {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} on {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}}.[[Taka-no-Miya#保育社カラー|カラーブックス伊勢神宮]]68頁『寝地蔵さん』[[Taka-no-Miya#矢野、杜|矢野、知られざる社]]53-54頁『荒御魂をまつる荒祭宮と多賀宮』。 On the approach to Taka-no-Miya, there is a stone known as the '''Sleeping Jizo-san''' (寝地蔵さん), which resembles {{ill|Jizo Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. For those with mobility difficulties, a '''Taka-no-Miya Remote Worship Area''' (多賀宮遥拝所) has been set up along the pond at the foot of the hill. Both {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} in the {{ill|Naiku|ja|内宮}} and Taka-no-Miya in the Geku, the foremost betsugu dedicated to the aramitama (wild spirit), lack {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居}} gates. The exact reason for this is unknown. == Deities == * '''Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami''' (豊受大御神荒魂)[[#保育社カラー|Color Books Ise Jingu]] p.69 'Betsugu: Tagamiya'[[#平凡辞典1940三巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1940), Vol. 3]] frame 23 (original p.31). : In the '''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''', '''Tenshōza Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Shidaiki''', and '''Ise Nishisho Kōtai Jingu Gochinza Denki''', the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami is also known as '''Ibukidonushi''' (伊吹戸主), born from the purification (禊) of '''Izanagi no Mikoto''' upon his return from '''Yomi'''[[#国史大系七|Kokushi Taikei, Vol. 7]] frame 263 (original p.499)[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 90 (original p.147), or alternatively as '''Kannaobi no Kami''' (神直毘神), another name for Ibukidonushi[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 142 (original p.239). == History == While the precise origins are unclear, it is said to have been established simultaneously with the founding of the Outer Shrine in the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign (478 CE)[[#皇大神宮史|Kōtai Jingu History]] frame 170 (original pp.242–243). The shrine was generally referred to as '''Takamiya''' (高宮), but after the Meiji period, the name was standardized to '''Tagamiya''' (多賀宮) based on the '''Engi Daijingu-shiki''', abolishing the former designation. The kanji 多賀宮 was likely chosen for its auspicious meaning. It has no connection to the similarly named '''Taga Taisha''' (多賀大社) or its deities Izanagi no Mikoto and Izanami no Mikoto. However, the Aramitama of Toyouke Ōmikami (the enshrined deity of Tagamiya) is also identified with Ibukidonushi, born from the purification of Izanagi no Mikoto[[#平凡辞典1937一巻|Shinto Daijiten (Heibon 1937), Vol. 1]] frame 232 (original p.398). Tagamiya once had a dedicated sacred fire hall (Imibiyaden), which was abolished during the Meiji period. During World War II, on July 29, 1945, the shrine's steps were damaged in the '''Ujiyamada Air Raid''', but the shrine building itself was saved by the efforts of the local guards[[#矢野、杜|Yano, Unknown Shrines]] p.184. == Rituals == Rituals are conducted similarly to those of the Outer Shrine, including the [[Kinen-sai]], [[Tsukinami-sai]], [[Kannamesai]], and [[Niiname-sai]], with offerings ('''heihaku''') from the Imperial Family. Imperial envoys visit Tagamiya following the Outer Shrine, making it the only Betsugu (detached shrine) of the Outer Shrine to receive this honor. This treatment is comparable to the {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|fr|Aramatsuri_no_miya|es|Aramatsuri no miya}} of the Inner Shrine[[#伊勢年鑑1942|Ise Yearbook (1942)]] frame 22 (original p.15). == Shrine Structure == The shrine building of Tagamiya[[#大神宮叢書.第3後篇|Daijingu Series (Vol. 3, Later Part)]] frames 359–360 (original pp.664–666), "Betsuin: Tagamiya Ichinomiya" follows the architectural style of the Outer Shrine, with '''chiselled-outward chigi''' (千木) and '''five odd-numbered katsuogi''' (鰹木) atop a thatched '''Shimmei-zukuri''' roof, facing south. The old site for the periodic shrine reconstruction (Shikinen Sengū) lies adjacent to the current grounds. == Access == * **Nearest Station:** Approximately 12-minute walk from [[JR Central|JR]] [[Sangū Line]] or [[Kintetsu]] [[Ise Line]] [[Ise-shi Station]]. * **Nearest Bus Stop:** About 5-minute walk from [[Mie Kōtsū]]'s Outer Shrine Front Bus Stop. * **Nearest Interchange:** Around 2 km from the [[Ise Expressway]] [[Ise Nishi Interchange]]. * **Parking:** Free parking available.
